widgets_values:
photorealistic clean animal reference image of the same animal from the reference image, single centered headshot, head and upper neck only, straight-on camera angle, clear readable expression, full unobstructed face, both eyes clearly visible, nose clearly visible, mouth clearly visible, ears clearly visible, no fur covering the eyes, no objects blocking the face, no person holding the animal, no hands, no leash, no collar, no harness, no clothing, no accessories, plain white studio background, even soft diffuse studio lighting, matte natural fur, realistic fur texture, clean well-defined facial features, natural proportions, realistic detail, clean professional reference photoshop_\(medium\)

nodes.title: MOODBOARD CHEAT SHEET
widgets_values:
- Prompt lives in the IDENTITY node. Moodboard prompt stays BLANK when fused
(only fill it if the Moodboard feeds the sampler directly, standalone t2i).

- Scene/style image -> Moodboard. Person -> Identity. Bypass Moodboard = identity-only.

- Strength: 0.5 balanced. 0.8-1.0 = follows scene more literally (layout can leak).
0.3 = colors/mood only.

- Content leaking from scene (objects, layout)? Escalate in order:
lower strength -> indirect ON -> 2x2 crops -> 4x4 tiles.

- Multiple refs or crops making grid/panel outputs? Turn indirect ON (always fixes it).

- extract "style" = palette/lighting/texture.  extract "subject" = composition/content
from the ref, prompt controls the look. Subject mode wants FULL image, no crops.

- vision_px: 1024 = richer detail from the ref, 384 = looser/vaguer vibe.

- Want the EXACT scene? Don't push strength - use the img2img latent path instead:
scene -> VAEEncode -> latent, denoise 0.65-0.75.

- A/B settings with a FIXED seed or you're comparing noise.

RECIPES
- Turbo: 8-12 steps, CFG 1, euler/simple. Negative unused at CFG 1.
- Raw / hard edits / removals: 20-40 steps, CFG 3 - negative node then matters:
keep it EMPTY prompt + SAME image(s) as positive.
- Raw loader: weight_dtype fp8_e4m3fn or the 24.5GB bf16 won't fit.

PERFORMANCE
- NEVER USE--highvram on the .bat file
for Krea 2 - Comfy hoards VRAM until it spills to RAM and crawls (35s/it symptom).
- Switching checkpoints around? Hit Unload Models, or old ones stay cached.

nodes.title: Moodboard Quick Notes
widgets_values:
Want the scene followed more literally? 
Raise moodboard strength toward 0.8–1.0 — accepting some layout leak — or flip the latent switch to the img2img path with the scene at ~0.7 denoise.

Want just the colors/mood with even less scene influence? 
Drop strength toward 0.3 or turn on indirect.

nodes.title: Person Extract Step 2
widgets_values:
Create a clean identity reference headshot from the input image.

Preserve the same person as closely as possible, including their sex presentation, ethnic appearance, skin tone, age range, face shape, eye shape, nose shape, mouth shape, jawline, hairline, and natural expression.

Show a centered front-facing head-and-upper-shoulders photo with the face looking straight at the camera. Make the face fully visible and unobstructed. Remove hats, glasses, masks, jewelry, hands, props, held items, and anything covering the face.

Use a soft light gray or off-white studio background instead of pure white. Use soft neutral studio lighting that keeps the facial color natural and does not wash out the face. Preserve natural facial color, subtle cheek redness, slight blush, minor blemishes, and normal skin variation. Keep the skin matte, dry-looking, low-shine, and realistic, with a clean dry nose area.

nodes.title: Krea 2 Turbo + Identity Edit - how to use
widgets_values:
TURBO settings (already set): 8 steps, CFG 1, euler/simple.

IDENTITY EDIT:
- Load a source image, type the instruction in the top encode node.
- The bottom encode node must keep an EMPTY prompt with the SAME image connected (grounded negative).
- Keep the Identity Edit LoRA loader ON for edits.
- Match the empty latent's aspect ratio roughly to the source; keep sources under ~2 MP.
- Removals work better on a Raw-based checkpoint: ~20 steps, CFG 3.
- Two sources: scene into 'image', subject into 'image2'.

PLAIN TXT2IMG:
Bypass (Ctrl+B) the LoadImage node AND the Identity Edit LoRA loader. The encode nodes then act as normal prompt boxes.

Your trained LoRA: copy checkpoints from E:\LORA MAKE\output into C:\Comfy Files\loras, pick in 'Your trained LoRA'. Bypass it to run without.

Other Krea 2 checkpoints swap into the top loader. If output is noisy at 8 steps, it's Raw-based: ~28 steps, CFG 3.5-5.

MOODBOARD FUSION (Neo recipe):
- Identity comes from the Subject image (resize -> Process 'image').
- Scene/vibe comes from the 'Edit source (scene)' image -> Moodboard node -> fuses into the Identity prompt.
- Balanced defaults: strength 0.5, extract style, before, directive ON.
- Content leaking from the scene (objects/layout)? Turn 'indirect' ON, or drop strength.
- Identity-only run: bypass (Ctrl+B) the Moodboard node - everything else keeps working.

nodes.title: Grounding Guide Note
widgets_values: Lower = stronger edit adherence; higher = stronger identity/likeness (768 balanced, 1024+ for people, 0 = native)

nodes.title: Person Extract Step 1
widgets_values:
Extract a clean image of the person from the input image while preserving the same identity as closely as possible.

Keep the same sex presentation, ethnic appearance, skin tone, age range, face shape, eye shape, nose shape, mouth shape, jawline, hairline, hairstyle, and natural expression. Keep the person looking as similar to the input as possible.

Remove anything that blocks or distracts from the person. Remove hats, glasses, masks, jewelry, hands, props, held objects, and any items covering the face or body. Keep only the person. Make the face fully visible and unobstructed.

Use a simple neutral background. Keep the lighting natural and balanced. Keep the skin matte, dry-looking, realistic, and not glossy. Preserve natural facial color, subtle cheek redness, slight blush, small blemishes, and normal skin variation. Do not beautify or average the face.

nodes.title: Prompt (2nd)
widgets_values:
Make a conservative cleaned version of the input image.

Keep the same person as close to the input image as possible. Preserve the exact facial structure, face shape, eyes, eyelids, eyebrows, nose shape, nostrils, mouth shape, lips, jawline, chin, cheek structure, hairline, hairstyle, skin tone, age appearance, sex presentation, ethnic appearance, and natural expression.

Do not redesign, beautify, average, smooth, or improve the person’s face. Do not change the person into a more attractive, younger, older, more masculine, more feminine, lighter-skinned, or darker-skinned version. Keep the face ordinary and specific to the input image.

Keep the same camera angle, same head angle, same facial expression, same lighting direction, and same overall facial proportions from the input image.

Only remove distractions that are not part of the person, such as objects, held items, background clutter, hats, glasses, masks, jewelry, or hands blocking the face. Remove them carefully without changing the person’s identity.

Place the person on a simple neutral background only if needed. Keep the lighting natural and close to the input image. Preserve natural facial color, subtle redness, blemishes, uneven skin tone, and normal skin variation. Keep the skin matte and realistic.

Photograph of a young Black woman with dark skin and braided hair standing against a white background. She wears a tight white t-shirt with short sleeves, white high-waisted jeans with multiple horizontal slashes revealing her skin, and black high-heeled sandals. Her right hand rests on her hip, and her left arm hangs by her side. She has pink lipstick and wears a small gold necklace with a cross pendant. Her expression is neutral, and she looks slightly to her left. The image has a watermark with the text "alamy" in multiple places. The background is completely white, making her the sole focus of the image.

Photograph of a narrow, winding path through a dense bamboo forest. The path is paved and slightly damp, with wooden railings on both sides. Tall, straight bamboo stalks with green leaves fill the background, creating a tunnel-like effect. The bamboo stalks are uniformly green with a bluish tint. Dry, brown grass grows on either side of the path, partially covering the lower parts of the railings. The lighting is natural, coming from above, suggesting a cloudy day. The vantage point is eye-level with the path. The image is safe for work (sfw) and does not include any people or animals. The overall atmosphere is serene and slightly cool due to the dense foliage.
